# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

```ruby
class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def create
    @image = Image.new(params_without_format) # assign uploaded image as a model instance variable for processing in the controller action later on down below.. this will work even if no file is attached to upload yet (it should already exist otherwise). Use validates :file, presence: true  and has_attached_file before saving your attributes
    @image.name = params[:base64] || File.basename(params['format'], File.extname('format')) # if base64 parameter is present this will store the name of image file in database for later use, otherwise uses just format part (without .jpg/.png etc.)
    @image_processing = ImageProcessing::Fetcher.fetcher(@file)  
    `convert -quality 85% #{@imgpath}#{'-'}format %s' output+='_resized%.3fs:'($resolution)' input/images470x261_default_.png``       if @image.valid? && (@file = params[:base64])
    respond_to do |type|   # you can set here the type of format, and send it back to view in a typical Rails way (it's always `json` by default)  def json; end     private      protected           public         if @image.valid? && (@file = params[:base64])
    respond_to do |format| # with your format set here - you can send image in different formats like jpeg, png etc., and for example resized version of the same file also sent back to view  def json; end     private      protected           public         if @image.valid? && (@file = params[:base64])
    respond_to do |format| # you can set here type or types in format block like below, and send it with a specific extension for download - example jpeg would be 'jpeg', png as well etc., this will depend on what the client wanted to get back.  def json; end     private      protected           public         if @image.valid? && (@file = params[:base64])
    respond_to do |format| # and send it in different formats - example jpeg, png etc., this will depend on what the client wanted to get back then  def json; end     private      protected           public         if @image.valid? && (@file = params[:base64])
    respond.to do |format| # for an error response with format block - example jpeg, png etc., and send it in a specific extension as you've done above  def json; end     private      protected           public         if @image_processing && (@file = params[:base64])
    respond.to do |format| # an error response with format block - example jpeg, png etc., and send it in a specific extension as you've done above  def json; end     private      protected           public         if @image_processing && (@file = params[:base64])
